item.title: A Holistic Market Research Analysis of Carbon-Negative Fuel Systems embedding Decision-Making Procedures
item.description:
Authors: Fatima Dargam, Gabriel Steffen

Source: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: The document is a short paper for ICDSST 2026 is authored by REACH Innovation together with several European research partners and presents the Market and Competitive Research Analysis (MCRA) developed within the Horizon Europe project NET‑Fuels. The paper explains how decision-support methodologies (SWOT, PESTEL, Porter’s Five Forces) were integrated into a structured workflow to evaluate and position NET‑Fuels’ carbon‑negative fuel technologies within the current technological and regulatory landscape.

item.title: A Holistic Market Research Analysis of Carbon-Negative Fuel Systems embedding Decision-Making Procedures
item.description:
Authors: Fatima Dargam, Gabriel Steffen

Source: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: The document is a short paper for ICDSST 2026 is authored by REACH Innovation together with several European research partners and presents the Market and Competitive Research Analysis (MCRA) developed within the Horizon Europe project NET‑Fuels. The paper explains how decision-support methodologies (SWOT, PESTEL, Porter’s Five Forces) were integrated into a structured workflow to evaluate and position NET‑Fuels’ carbon‑negative fuel technologies within the current technological and regulatory landscape.

item.title: Association between long-term exposure to ambient PM<sub>2</sub><sub>.</sub><sub>5</sub> and NO<sub>2</sub> and cognitive function in the CARTaGENE cohort.
item.description:
Authors: Olga Fliaguine, Dave Saint‐Amour, Maryse F. Bouchard, Nolwenn Noisel, Stéphane Buteau

Source: PubMed

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: INTRODUCTION: ) was associated with cognitive measures in the CARTaGENE cohort. METHOD: concentrations from satellite-based and land-use regression models, respectively. Associations with standardized cognitive scores were examined using mixed-effect models adjusted for potential confounders. Effect modification by sex, education, and smoking status was explored in subgroup analyses. RESULTS: and working memory were modified by the smoking status and education level. CONCLUSION: to poorer visual working memory.

item.title: Un Jumeau Numérique pour la Résilience d'un Système de Systèmes : Cas d’Application à une Ville Intelligente
item.description:
Authors: Meriem Smati, Christophe Danjou, Jannik Laval, Vincent Cheutet

Source: HAL (Le Centre pour la Communication Scientifique Directe)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: International audience

item.title: Virtual Exchange as an Equity, Diversity and Inclusion-compliant approach to English Language Teacher education
item.description:
Authors: Orsini-Jones, Marina; id_orcid 0000-0001-5250-5682, Chen, Yu-Hua; id_orcid 0000-0002-6350-6426, Koseoglu, Guray, Mkpayah, Patience, Suri, Preeti; id_orcid 0009-0001-3786-377X, Zhang, Kai

Source: Pure (Coventry University)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: This chapter discusses how virtual exchange (VE) projects can support female students on English language teacher education programmes, whose first language is not English, with reflecting on their beliefs, approaching their professional development in a critical way and feeling included and valued. Focusing in particular on the autoethnographical reflections of female participants who took part in VE projects that aimed at decolonising the English Language Teaching (ELT) curriculum, the chapter illustrates how VE can provide a powerful knowledge-sharing third space that enables female students to co-construct otherwise and pluralised ways of learning while also developing useful transversal competences.

item.title: Frequency-time weighted residuals: a discontinuous formulation for unilateral contact events
item.description:
Authors: Sophia McDonald, C. Legrand

Source: HAL (Le Centre pour la Communication Scientifique Directe)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: International audience

item.title: Jacques Lecoq’s Embodied and Ecological Actor Training
item.description:
Authors: Evans, Mark; id_orcid 0000-0003-0564-0771, Pearce, Michael, Kenney, Denise

Source: Pure (Coventry University)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution; Canada-related term in abstract: canada

Abstract: This chapter argues that Jacques Lecoq’s pedagogy, which nurtures creative expression through embodied identification with the more-than-human world and operates through non-hierarchical group creation, can be understood as a form of ecological enquiry, one that functions less as a fixed technique and more as an epistemology: a living methodology. <br/> <br/>Jacques Lecoq founded his school in Paris in 1956. Over the last seventy years, it has trained over 5,000 graduates from more than 80 countries, many of whom have gone on to teach and adapt his approach across the globe. Lecoq’s pedagogy offers students a distinctive journey of creative self-discovery. Unlike mainstream theatre programmes that centre on human psychological and social relations, Lecoq’s approach is predicated on the maxim tout bouge (‘everything moves’), a philosophy that invites students to experience their human...

item.title: Current knowledge and research gaps for manure management in sub-saharan Africa
item.description:
Authors: Sonja Leitner, Winnie Ntinyari, I. Wanyama, Mirja Michalscheck, Sven Gjedde; id_orcid 0000-0002-2673-0162 Sommer, David E. Pelster, Ngwa Martin Ngwabie, Lutz Merbold

Source: Unknown source

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

item.title: Did Recent Inflation Reflect a Nonlinear Phillips Curve?
item.description:
Authors: Paul Beaudry, Chenyu Hou, Franck Portier

Source: UCL Discovery (University College London)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: To examine the potential role of a nonlinear Phillips curve in explaining recent US inflation, we combine evidence from both crosscity variation and aggregate time series data.A central challenge relates to how best to control for the potential confounding effect of inflation expectations.The paper explores several options.Our findings place into question the robustness that tight labour markets, due to a nonlinear Phillips curve, played a very important role in the recent inflation episode.

item.title: YANG-Evolve: Agent-Orchestrated Semantic Backward Compatibility Verification for Evolving YANG Models
item.description:
Authors: Hesham ElAbd, Juergen Dingel, Robert Lee, Ali Tizghadam

Source: Zenodo (CERN European Organization for Nuclear Research)

Why included: Canadian-affiliated author or institution

Abstract: Artifacts and Experimental Scripts This repository contains the datasets, execution scripts, and result artifacts for the two primary experiments presented in our paper. Organization Note: All files and scripts are prefixed with a numbering system (e.g., 1.x for Component 1, 2.x for Component 2) to group related experiment components together. Note on Shared Dependencies: Both Component 1 and Component 2 rely on the comprehensive rule base provided in compatibility_rules.xml. Component 1: OpenConfig Commit Analysis This component evaluates commits and their related YANG files extracted from the OpenConfig GitHub repository. - Input Data: 1.4.workspace.zip (contains the extracted commits and YANG files organized into old/new directories for every pair).- Execution: We ran the 1.9.run_pyang_for_all_commits.py script across the workspace in two separate modes using the following commands:...
